title: Inability to code split inside for loop causes large output file

features.name: nested task calls with for loop
features.description: Code should include a for loop that calls a task, where that task calls another task, creating a nested call chain of at least 3 levels deep
features.snippet:
task inner(input int x); #1; endtask
task middle(input int x); #1; inner(x); endtask
task outer(input int x); #1; middle(x); endtask
initial for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) outer(i);

features.name: task with input argument called inside loop
features.description: Code should include a task that takes an input argument and is called from within a for loop in an initial block
features.snippet:
task write_data(input logic [31:0] data); #1; endtask
initial for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) write_data(32'h12345678);

features.name: chained task calls in nested hierarchy
features.description: Code should include multiple tasks where each task calls another task, forming a call chain of at least 4 levels
features.snippet:
task t1(input int x); #1; endtask
task t2(input int x); #1; t1(x); endtask
task t3(input int x); #1; t2(x); endtask
task t4(input int x); #1; t3(x); endtask
